title: Data Persistence Layer
description:
Implement a complete data persistence layer with strict isolation to enable easy migration from local storage to remote backend in the future. All data storage and retrieval must go through well-defined interfaces in dedicated modules.

**Architecture Goals:**
- Single source of truth for all persistence operations
- Abstract storage interface that hides implementation details
- Easy to swap from local (file/memory) to remote (database/API)
- Type-safe data contracts
- Clear separation of concerns

**Data to persist:**
- Sessions (active and historical)
- Problems and their specifications
- Student code revisions
- User preferences and settings
- Execution results (optional)

**Design Principles:**
1. Repository pattern: All data access through repository interfaces
2. No direct file system or database calls outside persistence modules
3. Async interfaces (even if local storage is synchronous)
4. Error handling and validation at persistence boundary
5. Migration path documented for future remote backend

**Initial Implementation:**
- Local file-based storage (JSON files)
- In-memory caching for active sessions
- Simple file structure in data/ directory

**Future Migration Path:**
- Replace file storage with REST API calls
- No changes to calling code (interface remains same)
- Configuration-based storage selection

**Modules to create:**
- src/server/persistence/ (new directory)
  - interfaces.ts - Storage interface definitions
  - local-storage.ts - File-based implementation
  - session-repository.ts - Session data operations
  - problem-repository.ts - Problem data operations
  - revision-repository.ts - Code revision operations

**Success Criteria:**
- All data operations isolated in persistence layer
- No file I/O or storage logic outside persistence modules
- Well-documented interfaces
- Easy to add new storage backend by implementing interface
